title: China-to-Argentina EV Charger Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: China GB/T 18487 and GB/T 20234 Evidence Limits
rows.target_requirement: For Argentina, China GB/T 18487 and GB/T 20234 reports should be treated as supporting technical evidence unless the Argentine buyer, engineer, utility, tender, or authority expressly accepts them. The Argentina-side file should still address IEC or IRAM references, Spanish documentation, local electrical installation, grid connection, connector interoperability, and site commissioning.
target_standards: IRAM or IEC references where requested
rows.china_equivalent: China's common EV conductive charging package includes GB/T 18487.1 for charging-system requirements and GB/T 20234 for connection devices. The 2023 GB/T 20234 updates include connection-device requirements for general and DC high-power charging interfaces, but they remain China-standard evidence rather than Argentina approvals.
rows.gap: Label GB/T reports as China technical evidence, then add an Argentina acceptance matrix showing which clauses or tests are accepted directly, which need IEC or IRAM retesting, which need Spanish documentation, and which are handled through utility or site commissioning.

mandatory_status.target: Argentina acceptance of GB/T evidence is voluntary, contractual, or authority-specific unless a binding Argentine instrument expressly accepts the China standard evidence
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487 and GB/T 20234 evidence is commonly required by China-market procurement or infrastructure practice; legal mandatory status depends on the exact Chinese rule or project instrument

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T reports are useful starting evidence for Argentina, not a passport. Build a separate Argentina acceptance matrix for IEC or IRAM references, connector choice, grid connection, and site commissioning.

source.publisher: Ministry of Industry and Information Technology of the People's Republic of China

rows.target_requirement: Argentina projects may specify IEC 62196 connector families, including Type 2 for AC charging or CCS Combo 2 for DC charging, through procurement documents, site engineering, fleet requirements, or network interoperability rules. A single mandatory national public-charger connector rule should not be assumed unless a binding Argentine instrument for the specific project says so.
rows.china_equivalent: China chargers and vehicles commonly use GB/T 20234 connector interfaces. GB/T 20234 AC and DC interfaces are not automatically interoperable with IEC 62196 Type 2 or CCS Combo 2 interfaces used in many IEC-based projects, so connector hardware, cable assemblies, communication, labeling, and user instructions may need Argentina-specific configuration.
rows.gap: Do not rely on a China GB/T plug drawing as proof of IEC 62196 interoperability. Confirm the Argentine fleet inlet type, public-network connector requirement, AC or DC communication route, cable ratings, locking behavior, and spare-parts plan before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary or project-specific unless mandated by tender, concession, public-network rule, fleet contract, utility condition, or another binding Argentine instrument
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T connector configuration is commonly expected for China-market equipment and infrastructure; mandatory status depends on the applicable Chinese product, procurement, or project route

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 20234 and IEC 62196 connector evidence should be treated as different interface families unless the exact Argentine project documents accept the China connector configuration.

rows.target_requirement: Argentina charger configurations should be validated for the local 50 Hz electricity system and for site voltage, phase, earthing, protection, metering, and distribution-service conditions. AC chargers, DC charger input stages, transformers, auxiliary power supplies, cooling systems, meters, and protection relays should be checked against the actual Argentine project supply before shipment and commissioning.
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic EV charger equipment is also commonly designed for 50 Hz operation, which reduces but does not eliminate export risk. Site voltage, plug configuration, grid code settings, earthing, protection, EMC, labeling, language, and distributor requirements still need Argentina-specific confirmation.
rows.gap: Provide Argentina configuration sheets showing 50 Hz ratings, input voltage tolerance, phase configuration, derating rules, protection settings, transformer compatibility, cooling-system ratings, and commissioning tests. Confirm that any China-only labels or software region settings are changed for Argentina.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as an engineering, safety, and utility-connection requirement where the Argentine supply is 50 Hz and site electrical conditions apply
mandatory_status.cn: China 50 Hz evidence is relevant but not enough without Argentina site-voltage and installation confirmation

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Frequency alone is not the main China-to-Argentina gap because both markets commonly use 50 Hz. The export file still needs Argentina site voltage, protection, earthing, labeling, and commissioning evidence.

rows.requirement: ENRE / Distributor Grid Connection and Load Review
rows.target_requirement: ENRE supervises federal-jurisdiction electricity service providers under Argentina's electricity framework and concession contracts. EV charger projects in ENRE-regulated distribution areas, especially higher-power AC or DC installations, may require distributor review for service capacity, metering, protection, power quality, civil works, and connection changes before energization.
rows.china_equivalent: China charger documentation may include GB/T charger tests, factory FAT records, and State Grid or local utility design assumptions. Those documents do not replace the Argentine distributor's site-specific load and connection approval for the actual point of supply.
china_standards: Chinese utility project connection documents where applicable
rows.gap: Prepare Argentina site load calculations, single-line diagrams, protection settings, earthing design, harmonic or power-quality evidence where relevant, metering details, and commissioning procedures for the distributor or engineer of record. Separate product compliance evidence from grid-connection approval.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the charger installation requires a new supply, increased contracted capacity, service modification, distributor approval, or ENRE-regulated connection condition
mandatory_status.cn: China grid-connection evidence is project-specific and does not approve an Argentine point of supply

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A GB/T charger report does not energize an Argentine site. Treat ENRE or distributor connection review as a separate project approval whenever the supply capacity or service connection is affected.

source.publisher: Ente Nacional Regulador de la Electricidad (ENRE), Argentina

rows.requirement: IRAM / IEC 61851 Conductive Charging Safety Reference
rows.target_requirement: IEC 61851-1:2017 is the international reference for EV conductive charging systems, including EV supply equipment operating characteristics, the EV-to-EVSE connection, and electrical safety requirements. In Argentina, IRAM or buyer specifications may reference IEC-based EVSE standards, but this should be treated as voluntary or project-specific unless an Argentine regulation, tender, utility condition, or electrical-installation approval expressly incorporates the requirement.
target_standards: IRAM-adopted or IRAM-referenced EVSE standards where specified by the project
rows.china_equivalent: China EV charger files commonly cite GB/T 18487.1 for conductive charging systems. The GB/T evidence can support technical review, but it is not automatically a substitute for IEC 61851 clause mapping or IRAM-referenced documentation requested by an Argentine buyer, engineer, utility, or authority.
rows.gap: Prepare a clause-by-clause bridge from GB/T 18487.1 test reports, EVSE ratings, protection functions, manuals, markings, and installation instructions to the requested IEC 61851 or IRAM-referenced clauses. Confirm the edition and whether the requirement is contractual, utility-driven, code-driven, or voluntary before presenting it as mandatory.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary or project-specific unless incorporated by Argentine regulation, utility condition, tender, contract, or electrical-installation approval
mandatory_status.cn: Recommended national standard evidence unless made mandatory by Chinese procurement, project approval, local inspection, or another binding instrument

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 18487 evidence should be mapped to IEC 61851 or IRAM-referenced clauses when Argentina project documents ask for IEC-style EVSE safety evidence.

rows.requirement: Argentina EV Policy Context vs Charger Approval
rows.target_requirement: Argentina energy-transition and mobility policy materials support cleaner transport and mobility electrification, but policy direction is not the same as a charger product approval. For a charger export file, separately confirm import classification, electrical safety, installation permits, tax or incentive eligibility, consumer labeling, and grid-connection obligations.
target_standards: Argentina national energy transition planning materials
Argentina EV import, tax, customs, and incentive instruments where applicable
Local installation and utility approval rules
rows.china_equivalent: China new-energy vehicle and charging-infrastructure policy support may help explain the product's domestic market context. It does not establish Argentina import eligibility, grid acceptance, subsidy eligibility, or electrical-installation approval.
china_standards: China new-energy vehicle charging infrastructure policy documents
rows.gap: Build the Argentina market-access file around binding requirements, not policy headlines. Track whether each item is a law, regulation, customs classification, tax incentive, public tender condition, private buyer requirement, grid requirement, or voluntary standard.

mandatory_status.target: Policy context is informational; specific legal, customs, tax, grid, tender, or permit obligations become mandatory only through the applicable binding instrument
mandatory_status.cn: China policy support is informational for Argentina unless separately accepted by an Argentine buyer or authority

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Argentina EV policy signals should not be presented as charger certification. Convert policy references into a separate checklist of binding import, electrical, grid, and project obligations.
